rationale: Choose C+B: use the version-portable quiet existence probe so genuine deletion never refuses, but do not claim it classifies infrastructure errors. Ground the gate's store-wide fail-closed behavior in ApproveImpl's already-prior MergeBase call, and pin the single-corrupt-ref residual with real-git tests showing that FinalizeEpic cannot merge that ref (merge fails, or no matching worktree is enumerated). This closes the actual risk—an unsettled orphan being auto-merged—without another brittle exit-code classifier. Approach A is technically excellent on Git >=2.43, but that floor is not reasonable to introduce silently here: README/user guides require only Git with worktree support, installers do not check a Git version, and CI has no minimum-Git job. Official Git 2.42 documentation lacks --exists while 2.43 documents the 0/2/1 contract, so A would exclude still-plausible installations and require a project-wide compatibility decision. Tests should assert deleted-branch passage, whole-store failure before scanning, and no merge/terminal mutation for a corrupt enumerated ref; they must not fabricate a per-ref absent/error distinction on pre-2.43 Git.

rejected.A: Correct 0/2/1 primitive, but requires an undocumented and unenforced Git >=2.43 product floor.
rejected.B: A new generic health probe adds surface and still cannot prove every individual ref healthy; reuse the existing MergeBase signal instead.
rejected.C: Pure C is safe, but the existing MergeBase precondition and residual integration tests should be made explicit rather than left only as narrative.
rejected.D: Worktree alignment is attractive but changes the shared epic-scoped predicate and has unnecessary blast radius for this fix.

structural_checks.worktree_list_with_unattached_corrupt_re: exit 0; corrupt bead ref not enumerated

version_floor.evidence: Official git-show-ref 2.42.0 documentation has no --exists form; 2.43.0 documentation adds --exists and specifies present=0, missing=2, lookup-error=1.
version_floor.project_floor: No Git version floor found; README and user docs state only Git/Git worktree support, installers do not version-check Git, and CI does not exercise a minimum Git version.
